note: Create the canonical user-facing runtime flow model and close Stage 1 of the operator journey: install, init, bootstrap, readiness gating, bounded remediation, and the transition to lawful non-bootstrap execution.

reason: tighten Stage-1 init law around root bootstrap carriers so init must create framework-owned AGENTS.md when absent, create AGENTS.sidecar.md when absent, and normalize pre-existing project-specific AGENTS content into the sidecar rather than leaving competing bootstrap law in root scope.

add the known Stage-2 operator flow for project activation and configuration: explicit vs automatic mode, lifecycle operations, validation/fail-closed behavior, protocol promotion posture, and bounded config/sync status surfaces.
link the operator-flow model to the new bootstrap-carrier and project-activator model as the detailed owner for orchestrator-init, agent-init, and project-activator onboarding routing.
add the known Stage-3 operator flow for intake and planning: bounded scope/PBI discussion, intake normalization, spec/task-graph formation, planning queries, and explicit re-baselining on drift.

add the known Stage-4 operator flow for execution, approval, and interrupt-resume: tracked execution progression, approval gates, explicit resumable waits, optional between-task approval mode, and replay-safe continuation boundaries.
ground Stage-4 interrupt/resume semantics in the promoted execution approval and interrupt-resume survey and make durable, bounded, inspectable continuation an explicit research-backed rule.

add the `execution_preparation` planning mode with `solution_architect` as the default lane and require an architecture-preparation report plus developer handoff packet before code-shaped execution when that task class requires it.
